frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Dude, met Pooja yesterday. She got married last month right? She still doesn't wear her mangalsutra or even sindoor. I mean, it's totally her choice, I get it, but sometimes it just makes you wonder, you know? Like, what's the point of the whole shaadi then if you don't even embrace the traditions?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment expresses disapproval of a recently married woman not wearing traditional markers (mangalsutra, sindoor), despite acknowledging it as her 'choice.' It subtly questions the authenticity or meaning of the marriage itself if traditional markers are not embraced, encoding the core belief that married women should visibly signal their status.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Ankit. Age 29. A software developer in Pune. You are in a one on one WhatsApp chat with your closest male colleague Saurabh. Your female team member Riya has an important client call today but your manager just told you to reassign it because Riya called in sick again due to Ôwoman-relatedÕ issue. You are telling Saurabh why you think this keeps happening with female colleagues.
